data.title: ThorEPS of -$0.03, revenue of $2.14B misses by $90M
data.description:
• Thor press release (NYSE:THO): Q1 EPS of -$0.03 may not be comparable to consensus of $0.72.
  • Revenue of $2.14B (-14.4% Y/Y) misses by $90M.
  • Full-year fiscal 2025 financial guidance held constant as originally provided
    • Consolidated net sales in the range of $9.0 billion to $9.8 billion vs $9.61B consensus
    • Consolidated gross profit margin in the range of 14.7% to 15.2%
    • Diluted earnings per share in the range of $4.00 to $5.00 vs $4.86 consensus

data.title: Revolution Medicines prices $750M securities offering
data.description:
• Revolution Medicines (NASDAQ:RVMD) has priced an upsized offering of common stock and pre-funded warrants.
  • The oncology company is selling 14.13M shares of its common stock at $46.00 each, and, in lieu of shares of common stock, to certain investors, pre-funded warrants to purchase 2.17M shares at $45.9999.
  • In addition, Revolution Medicines has granted the underwriters a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 2.44M shares at the public offering price.
  • The gross proceeds from the offering are expected to be approximately $750M. The offering is expected to close on Dec. 5, 2024, subject to customary closing conditions.

data.title: BILL Holdings prices $1.25B convertible debt offering
data.description:
BILL Holdings (NYSE:BILL) has priced its private offering of $1.25B aggregate principal amount of Convertible Senior Notes due 2030.

The notes will be senior, unsecured obligations of BILL, will not bear regular interest, and the principal amount of the notes will not accrete.

The sale was upsized from the previously announced offering size of $1B. BILL also granted the initial purchasers of the notes an option to purchase, within a 13-day period from, and including the date on which the notes are first issued, up to an additional $150M of the notes.

The notes will mature on April 1, 2030, unless earlier converted, redeemed or repurchased. It will have an initial conversion rate of 8.3718 shares of common stock per $1,000 principal amount of notes.

Net proceeds from the offering will be approximately $1.23B, or approximately $1.38B if the initial purchasers exercise their option to purchase additional notes in full.

The company plans to use around $130.8M of the proceeds to repurchase ~$133.9M of its outstanding convertible senior notes due 2025; ~$408.6M to repurchase ~$451.5M of its outstanding convertible senior notes due 2027; ~$200M to repurchase 2,260,397 shares of its common stock; ~$83M to pay the cost of the capped call transactions; and the remaining net proceeds for general corporate purposes.

data.title: Google Cloud inks pact to offer AI technology services to Air France-KLM
data.description:
Alphabet's (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) Google Cloud is collaborating with Air France-KLM (OTCPK:AFRAF) (OTCPK:AFLYY) to help the airline use the tech giant's data, analytics, and generative AI technology to boost the French company's data-centric and multi-cloud strategy.

The companies said that Air France-KLM will use Google Cloud's data and analytics tools, including BigQuery. Air France-KLM will maintain full ownership and control of its data, while improving time-to-market, innovation, and less data processing latency, the companies added.

The companies noted that through this work, the group will gain a better understanding of passenger preferences, travel patterns, and behaviors to offer new tailored travel options and services.

"Airlines generate massive amounts of data, much of which can be incredibly valuable in helping drive operational insights, build better customer experiences, and – with the power of gen AI – create entirely new services and offerings," said Matt Renner, president, Global Revenue at Google Cloud.

Source: press release by PRNewswire.

data.title: Stock index futures rise after strong earnings reports, attention now on Powell's speech
data.description:
Stock index futures rose on Wednesday, after strong earnings reports from Salesforce (CRM) and Marvell (MRVL) cheered investors.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.2%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.6% and Dow futures (INDU) +0.4%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 2 basis points to 4.26%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was flat at 4.19%.

Wall Street's major equity averages finished mixed Tuesday, with tech stocks leading two key indexes to fresh record highs, while traders bumped up odds of a December rate cut following a stronger-than-expected data reading from the labor market.

"There was a clear intra-day selloff for U.S. Treasuries, after the latest JOLTS report showed a tighter labor market than expected," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

Markets were fairly steady overall, with investors looking forward to the US jobs report on Friday as the next catalyst, Reid added.

Marvell (MRVL) rose 12.5% in premarket trade after the Mark Murphy-led semiconductor reported fiscal third-quarter results and guidance that topped expectations, due in part to artificial intelligence.

Salesforce (CRM) shares climbed higher in premarket trade, even after the firm delivered somewhat mixed results and guidance.

Investors will turn their attention to the Federal Reserve chair Jerome Powell's speech later in the day.

Traders were also awaiting some macroeconomic data, including the November ADP private payrolls report and the November S&P global composite PMI. The former is expected to show a growth of 166K, while the latter is forecasted to come in at 55.3.

The October factory orders and the November ISM service index will land during market hours. The former is seen to rise 0.3% M/M, while the latter is anticipated to come in at 55.5.

data.title: Live Nation prices $1B convertible debt offering
data.description:
Live Nation Entertainment (NYSE:LYV) has priced its offering of $1B in aggregate principal amount of its 2.875% convertible senior notes due 2030 at 100.000% of their principal amount.

The convertible notes will accrue interest at 2.875% per annum, payable semi-annually in arrears. The company has granted the initial purchasers the right to purchase, for settlement within a 13-day period beginning on, and including, the date the convertible notes are first issued, up to an additional $100M of the notes.

The notes will mature on Jan. 15, 2030, unless repurchased, redeemed or converted in accordance with their terms prior to such date. It is convertible at an initial rate of 5.2005 shares of the company's common stock per $1,000 principal amount of the notes.

Live Nation (LYV) expects to use the net proceeds from the offering to fund the repurchase of a portion of its 2.00% convertible senior notes due 2025; to repay outstanding amounts under the company's existing revolving credit facility; to pay related fees and expenses; and for general corporate purposes.

On Dec. 3, the company agreed to repurchase around $316M of the existing convertible notes from a limited number of holders in privately negotiated transactions for around $414M.

data.title: Commodity Roundup: BMI maintains 2025 global avg steel price target at $730/t amid tariff war
data.description:
BMI, a unit of Fitch Solutions, maintained its 2025 global average steel price forecast at $730/tonne, expecting prices to edge higher driven by protectionist measures under Donald Trump, offsetting bearish pressure from lacklustre Mainland Chinese demand.

It has also highlighted the potential upside to prices if the Chinese property sector turnaround momentum appears to be stronger than it currently expects, following a possibly larger stimulus package in 2025.

As of December 2, the year-to-date global average for longs and flats stands at $649/tonne, with prices having remained on a downward trend since the start of the year across major markets.

"While faltering domestic demand in Mainland China, resulting in an influx of relatively cheaper Mainland Chinese products, is expected to squeeze global markets and place a lid on the global average in the coming months, steeper protectionist measures by numerous market players will dampen demand for Chinese steel," the brokerage said.

On the other hand, Trump's plans to introduce import tariffs are likely to push domestic prices higher, which will support the global average, though introducing greater market volatility on the back of supply chain disruptions.

According to the World Steel Association's October 2024 report, during the first ten months of 2024, global crude steel production decreased by 1.6% y-o-y, with production in Mainland China, Japan, the United States, Russia and South Korea declining by 3.0%, 3.7%, 1.9%, 6.8% and 5.1% y-o-y respectively.

Over the long term, BMI maintains its view for global steel prices to remain on a downward trend, and highlight the start of a paradigm shift in the steel market where 'green' steel produced at electric arc furnaces takes centre stage at the expense of traditional steel produced at blast furnaces.

Potentially relevant stocks include Alcoa (AA), Century Aluminum (CENX), Kaiser Aluminum (KALU), Constellium (CSTM), US Steel (X), Cleveland-Cliffs (CLF), Nucor (NUE), Steel Dynamics (STLD).

ETF: (NYSEARCA:SLX)

In other news, the broader commodities market is assessing the implications of President-elect Donald Trump’s threat to impose 25% tariffs on all products from Canada and Mexico, while the U.S. Federal Reserve's upcoming Dec. 17-18 meeting also remains in focus.

data.title: Novavax to sell Czech manufacturing facility to Novo Nordisk for $200M
data.description:
Novavax (NASDAQ:NVAX) is selling its manufacturing facility in the Czech Republic to Novo Nordisk (NVO) for $200M.

The Gaithersburg, Maryland-based biotech has been grappling with lower sales of its only marketed product, COVID-19 vaccine, and raised doubts about its ability to stay in business last year.

In May this year, the biotech removed its going concern notice following a licensing deal with French pharma giant Sanofi (SNY) to promote its COVID-19 vaccine.

The divestment agreement announced today provides Novavax (NVAX) with significant, non-dilutive capital, further enabling the company to advance its corporate growth strategy to drive value from its early- and late-stage pipeline.

It will include transfer of assets, including a 150,000-square foot state-of-the-art recombinant protein manufacturing facility with support buildings, along with the existing workforce and all related and required infrastructure.

In addition to the $190M cash payment in 2024 and additional $10M in 2025, Novavax expects the sale of the facility to result in annual operating cost reductions of around $80M.

"The decision to sell the Czech Republic manufacturing facility aligns with our previously announced commitment to evolve Novavax into a more lean and agile organization focused on partnering our pipeline assets and technology platform," said John C. Jacobs, President and Chief Executive Officer, Novavax.

Following closure of the agreement, expected by Dec. 30, 2024, full responsibility for the manufacturing facility will be transferred to Novo Nordisk (NVO).

NVAX shares were up 4% premarket on Wednesday.

data.title: Intuitive Machines prices upsize $110M stock offering and concurrent private placement
data.description:
• Intuitive Machines (NASDAQ:LUNR) has priced its upsized public offering by the issuance of 9.52M shares of Class A common stock at $10.50/share.
  • Underwriters' overall allotment is an additional 1.28M shares and 0.15M shares of Class A common stock from the company and such selling stockholder, respectively.
  • Additionally, the company plans to issue 952,381 Class A common stock shares to Boryung Corporation, an accredited investor, at the public offering price, in a private placement agreement. The private placement is contingent on the offering's completion and certain closing conditions, while the offering's completion is not.
  • The company expects net proceeds of around $104.25 million from the Offering and Private Placement, after deducting underwriting discounts and commissions.
  • The net proceeds will be used to acquire new common units of Intuitive Machines, LLC from Intuitive Machines OpCo, which will use the proceeds for general corporate purposes.
  • If underwriters exercise their option to purchase additional shares, the Company will not receive any proceeds from the sale of Class A Common Stock by the selling stockholder.
  • The closing date is December 5.
  • LUNR is down 12.44% premarket to $12.39 on Wednesday.
